channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This app sends faxes for free from your iPhone, Android device, Mac, or PC
item.description: Nowadays, when you hear someone talk about faxing, there’s a decent chance it’s the punchline to a groan-inducing dad joke. (Not that I would ever be guilty of such silliness, of course. I stick strictly to the fax.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Musk’s crew descends on CFPB with plan to shut it down
item.description: The CFPB is funded independently through the Federal Reserve, but the CFPB director has the power to determine how much money the agency needs. That means a CFPB director could zero out the agency's budget entirely.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg News, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta’s Ray-Ban smart glasses are having their Super Bowl moment
item.description: It’s game time for Meta’s wearables: The tech giant has bought two ad spots for its Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses during Sunday’s Super Bowl broadcast, including one that has Chris Hemsworth and Chris Pratt wreaking havoc on Kris Jenner’s art collection.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Lawmakers turn to IGs to investigate DOGE access to agency data
item.description: Democrats are turning to inspector general's offices, including those swept up in Trump's firing of several IG's last month, to investigate DOGE's data access. The post Lawmakers turn to IGs to investigate DOGE access to agency data first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk pledges to rehire young DOGE member after recent racist posts
item.description: The work DOGE has drawn fury from Democrats and charges that it is illegally accessing the private data of millions of Americans, and violating the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: This open source tool could save Google, Microsoft, billions by cutting CPU resources by 20% but even small businesses can benefit
item.description: Yandex's new Perforator tool can save firms like Google and Microsoft billions, but even small businesses can benefit from it.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Severance director Ben Stiller says work is underway on season 3's scripts – and he hopes it won't take three years to make
item.description: Apple hasn't renewed Severance for a third season yet, but its creative team is already hard at work on developing it.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Transportation Department suspends $5 billion EV charger program
item.description: The Federal Highway Administration (FHA) on Thursday announced the suspension of a Biden-era electric vehicle (EV) charging network, the latest in a series of moves by the Trump administration to gut its predecessors’ energy and environmental moves.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, The Hill

item.title: The Hidden Dangers of Abandoned Cloud Infrastructure
item.description: A recent investigation by watchTowr highlights the risks posed by abandoned or poorly configured cloud infrastructure, revealing a widespread blind spot that urgently needs industry attention.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Full-stack JavaScript leads the way
item.description: Building web apps begins with a simple premise, but the real-world experience is a juggling act of optimizations, best practices, toolchains, and so on. Developers tend to respond by doing what we do best: creating new tools and techniques to manage ever-changing demands.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Hundreds of rigged votes can skew AI model rankings on Chatbot Arena, study finds
item.description: The generative AI revolution has turned into a global race, with mixtures of models from private companies and open-source initiatives all competing to become the most popular and powerful.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Connolly knocks Trump, Musk over 'McCarthy Era 2.0' for government employees
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly (D-Va.) on Thursday knocked President Trump and tech billionaire Elon Musk over what he called a new McCarthy Era for government employees.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge blocks Trump plan for federal worker resignations
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk, one of President Donald Trump's most powerful advisers, has orchestrated an unprecedented financial incentive for people to leave their government jobs, promising several months of pay in return for their resignation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: House lawmakers push to ban AI app DeepSeek from US government devices
item.description: Lawmakers in the House are proposing to ban the Chinese artificial intelligence app DeepSeek from U.S. government devices.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fortinet targets branch offices with upgraded firewalls
item.description: Fortinet has refreshed its branch office firewalls, adding power and features to its next-generation FortiGate G-series devices.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Airlines are finally embracing Apple’s AirTags—which means lost luggage could be a thing of the past
item.description: There’s nothing more annoying than arriving at your destination and finding that your checked baggage didn’t make the trip. But thanks to Apple’s new partnership with 15 different airlines, it’s easier than ever to track down your lost luggage—provided you have the right $29 gadget.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Try these tips to help your parents stay safe online
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Realtor.com leaves Santa Clara to set up headquarters in Texas
item.description: Damian Eales, chief executive officer of Realtor.com, said Texas’s growing talent pool, low cost of living and expansion of the housing stock were part of its appeal.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: The swarm of earthquakes shaking Santorini prompts Greece to declare an emergency
item.description: Greece has declared a state of emergency on Santorini because of the hundreds of undersea earthquakes that have shaken the resort island for nearly a week.
item.dc:creator: Derek Gatopoulos, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lawmakers warn hiring freeze could thwart cyber workforce efforts
item.description: A former White House official who led cyber and tech recruiting efforts said the hiring freeze "will set us back months if not years." The post Lawmakers warn hiring freeze could thwart cyber workforce efforts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Why I want glasses that are always listening
item.description: The trouble with virtual assistants is that they’re just so darn needy. Specifically, they need to be told exactly what to do. They sit around doing nothing until explicitly directed. They don’t take any initiative. They’re, for lack of a better word, lazy.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Using NATS with .NET Aspire
item.description: If you’re building a cloud-native application using a scalable cluster of microservices, you’re going to need a way for those services to communicate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5
